description_long: This object is a compact, boxy submachine pistol resembling the iconic MAC-10, featuring a rectangular steel frame with a prominent magazine well extending downward. The exterior is finished in a dark, worn metal with visible scratches and signs of use, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The barrel is short and cylindrical, protruding slightly from the front, with a simple muzzle and no visible compensator or suppressor attached. The grip is straight and angular, constructed from the same metal as the frame, and the trigger guard is squared and robust, providing ample space for finger placement. The top of the frame includes a cocking knob and minimalistic iron sights, while the magazine is long and rectangular, matching the industrial aesthetic. There are no visible engravings or decorative markings, emphasizing the weapon's functional, no-nonsense design.
description: A compact, boxy submachine pistol with a dark, worn metal finish, a long rectangular magazine, and a utilitarian, industrial design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the rectangular frame, long magazine, and squared trigger guard.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the short barrel, muzzle opening, and the width of the frame.
description_view_2: Top-rear view displaying the cocking knob, iron sights, and the magazine's alignment.
description_view_3: Rear-bottom view focusing on the back of the frame and the base of the magazine.
attribute_grip_design: The grip is straight and angular, constructed from the same dark, worn metal as the rest of the frame. It has a utilitarian, industrial look with no visible texturing, checkering, or ergonomic finger grooves. The surface appears smooth but slightly rough due to wear, and there are no logos, embellishments, or unique design elements present.
attribute_trigger_guard_style: The trigger guard is squared and robust, with a thick, boxy profile that matches the overall industrial design of the handgun. It is generously sized, providing ample space for finger placement, and lacks any undercuts, reinforcement, or decorative features. The edges are straight and functional, with no visible texturing or integrated accessories.
attribute_barrel_type: The barrel is short and cylindrical, protruding slightly from the front of the frame. It has a plain, unadorned profile with no visible threading, porting, or compensator. The finish is consistent with the rest of the weapon, showing signs of wear and a matte, dark coloration. No external markings or special features are apparent on the barrel.
attribute_slide_markings: There are no visible markings, engravings, or inscriptions on the slide. The surface is plain and utilitarian, with only minor wear and scratches present, and no manufacturer logos, model names, serial numbers, or decorative patterns are apparent.
attribute_magazine_base_shape: The magazine base is flat and rectangular, matching the long, boxy shape of the magazine itself. It lacks any flaring, beveling, or finger rests, and there are no visible logos, texturing, or embellishments. The design is simple and functional, in keeping with the overall utilitarian aesthetic.
